description: The canonical machine-readable AFI District / API Atlas foundation (v0.1). Descriptive discoverability layer over the current protocol: two active Districts, their capabilities, the structures that implement them, the real current interfaces, typed routes, referenced contracts, repository roles, participant roles, and onboarding descriptors. Every operational/partial entity cites implementation + test evidence. The Atlas describes protocol truth; it executes, routes, resolves, activates, and persists nothing (ATLAS-GOV D-ATLAS-1).
sourceOfTruthOrder: accepted governance (afi-governance/decisions) — owns authority and District boundaries
canonical schemas/contracts (afi-config, afi-math) — own machine-valid shape and invariants
current implementation and tests (afi-reactor, afi-infra, afi-core, afi-gateway, afi-tiny-brains, afi-factory, afi-token) — prove operational reality
this Atlas registry — assembles discoverability and relationships
the future AFI Protocol City — later visualizes and navigates the Atlas

districts.name: Signal Evaluation
districts.purpose: AFI's active Signal Evaluation capability and authority domain: canonical signal input -> five-category provider-backed enrichment -> deterministic join -> analyst/scorer/UWR seam -> the scored evaluation result handed to District Two, without becoming a source of financial truth.

evidence.note: the one GraphExecutor; sole signal-evaluation executor
all five lanes fire; one result per category

districts.name: Evidence and Provenance
districts.purpose: AFI's active Evidence and Provenance capability and authority domain: invocation-proof binding, canonical Evidence V3 record construction, canonical record and replay hashing, the append-once persistence admission boundary, and replay/supersession semantics. District Two never invokes a provider; evidence describes the invocation that occurred.

districts.name: Settlement, Rewards, Reputation & On-Chain Value
districts.purpose: RESERVED capability-domain candidate (not an active District): the future domain of epoch settlement, reward tracks, reputation, and on-chain value. Recorded exactly as authority-districts-v0.1 Part D.2 and R1-GOV D-R1-3 already record it -- an unnumbered, uncreated candidate that requires its own governance (CHAIN-GOV) before it becomes a District. No current capability is owned here.
authorityRefs: R1-GOV D-R1-3 (future-districts version-expansion roster; re-records the former §13 roster authority)
districts.constructionSummary: afi-token is deployed on Base Sepolia testnet (86B hard cap, role-gated emissions); afi-mint's off-chain orchestration is implemented and parity-tested but its on-chain execution is not wired; AFI Settlement v1 is governed doctrine with no contracts implemented; the Provider/Analyst/Validator reward-track closure is draft, not accepted. The owning District/authority is reserved to CHAIN-GOV.

capabilities.name: Signal ingest and canonical normalization
capabilities.purpose: Receive authorized signal input at an ingress boundary (USS v1.1 webhook or the CPJ community-provider-journal route), resolve the provider->strategy binding fail-closed, map to the canonical USS v1.1 form, and validate before evaluation.

capabilities.name: Five-category provider-backed enrichment
capabilities.purpose: Execute exactly the five governed enrichment categories (technical, pattern, sentiment, news, aiMl) through the provider-adapter runtime, resolving an explicit ProviderInstance per lane, validating each governed afi.enrichment.<category>.v1 output, and joining exactly one result per category. All five lanes must succeed for a scored evaluation to exist.

capabilities.name: Deterministic analyst scoring and UWR
capabilities.purpose: Invoke the registered analyst strategy and the canonical afi-core UWR scorer (invoked, never re-implemented) under the pinned UWR profile law, producing the scored evaluation result at the scorer/UWR seam handed to District Two.

capabilities.name: Pipeline composition authoring
capabilities.purpose: Author, validate, inspect, and hash the governed signal-evaluation compositions (pipeline manifests, analyst-strategy configs, analysis plugins) that District One executes. A District One (Signal Evaluation domain) capability governed by the FCP-GOV composition model and implemented by the replaceable Factory authoring structure (SLOT-FCP-FACTORY); Factory produces artifacts and executes nothing.

capabilities.name: Canonical Evidence V3 construction
capabilities.purpose: Construct the one canonical afi.scored-signal-evidence.v3 record from the scored evaluation result and the five carried provider-invocation proofs: bind invocation proofs, cross-check every identity and hash fail-closed, and compute the recordHash/replayHash commitments. Implemented in afi-reactor's District Two evidence law (src/evidence/); the record shape is the afi-config governed contract.

capabilities.name: Canonical evidence persistence (sole writer)
capabilities.purpose: The sole canonical evidence write path: V3-only admission, recordHash/replayHash verified by recomputation before insert, a unique signalId index, append-once/idempotent submit, and transactional supersede that refuses a finalized record. afi-infra is the single writer; Reactor and Gateway are submitters, never writers.

structures.name: AFI Reactor runtime
structures.purpose: The one manifest-driven GraphExecutor host. Serves District One (signal ingest, five-category enrichment, deterministic scoring) and District Two (the src/evidence/ Evidence V3 construction law). Boot is a fail-closed gate over the afi-config registries; it is a submitter to afi-infra, never a writer.

structures.name: afi-core scoring library
structures.purpose: The canonical in-process scoring library: the UWR aggregator (structure/execution/risk/insight axes clamped to [0,1]), the fail-closed UWR profile loader, and the decay/Greeks templates. Invoked by the Reactor scorer node; delegates numeric decay to afi-math.

structures.name: afi-math deterministic kernels
structures.purpose: The governed sole-source deterministic off-chain math authority (math-authority-v0.1): decay/Greeks kernels consumed by afi-core scoring (District One) and the 86-billion emissions schedule consumed by the reserved settlement domain. Golden-vector pinned; a diff requires governance review.

structures.name: AFI Factory authoring system
structures.purpose: The replaceable pipeline authoring system (SLOT-FCP-FACTORY, FCP-GOV D-FCP-4): 14 agent-facing operations over one dispatch, projected onto five surfaces (TypeScript SDK, CLI, capability catalog, tool definitions, MCP stdio adapter). It produces artifacts and executes no pipeline; it is not the API Atlas, not the Gateway, not the Reactor.

evidence.note: authoring system; nothing executes a pipeline

structures.name: AFI Gateway
structures.purpose: The external submission boundary: authenticates the API key, resolves the tenant, rate-limits, does a presence-only check, stamps provenance (providerId=gateway:<tenantId>), forwards to the Reactor, and returns the answer verbatim. It routes; it never writes canonical evidence and does not own the signal-evaluation capability.

structures.name: AFI Tiny Brains service
structures.purpose: The bounded first-party self-hosted aiMl orchestration + deterministic pattern kernel service serving District One's aiMl and pattern lanes. Runs the ProviderInstance-selected orchestration profile of approved experts through a deterministic resolver, fail-closed; its result is read-only context and does not affect UWR scoring.

structures.name: afi-infra canonical evidence store
structures.purpose: The sole canonical evidence writer (MongoScoredSignalEvidenceStore): a typed library dependency (no HTTP/API surface), V3-only admission, recordHash/replayHash verified by recomputation before insert, unique signalId index, append-once/idempotent, transactional supersede refusing finalized records.

evidence.note: No HTTP/API surface is exported (ATLAS-GOV, out of scope)

structures.name: afi-config governed contracts and registries
structures.purpose: The normative home of the governed schemas, registries, KATs, and conventions the runtime consumes -- including this Atlas registry. It is the canonical contract/schema authority where governance delegates it; it holds the schema boundaries the Districts own.

evidence.note: governed JSON schema boundaries (schemas/) + the registries/
governed registries incl. this Atlas registry

structures.name: afi-governance decision ledger
structures.purpose: The canonical home of accepted protocol-development decisions. It defines authority and District boundaries; it holds the authority the Atlas describes. It is not runtime and has no CI (acceptance is owner merge).

evidence.note: the accepted decision chain (18 decisions incl. ATLAS-GOV)

structures.name: afi-docs documentation hub
structures.purpose: The single authoritative human-readable current architecture map (AFI_Full_Architecture.md) and the mechanically drift-checked Atlas summary. Documentation explains authority; it does not hold it.

evidence.note: the single current architecture map, guarded

structures.name: AFI on-chain token
structures.purpose: The xERC20-based AFIToken with an on-chain 86-billion hard cap enforced in its sole role-gated emissions entrypoint, plus the signal-receipt and mint-coordinator contracts. Deployed on Base Sepolia testnet only (addresses documented, no committed broadcast artifacts); no mainnet. Serves the reserved settlement domain; its District/authority is reserved to CHAIN-GOV.

structures.name: afi-mint off-chain orchestration
structures.purpose: The off-chain TypeScript mint/emissions orchestration (signal-state, challenge-window, mint executor) parity-tested against the afi-math 86B schedule. On-chain execution is not wired (the executor targets an abstract interface; the Solidity contracts are empty stubs). Serves the reserved settlement domain; reserved to CHAIN-GOV.

structures.name: afi-benchkit benchmark harness
structures.purpose: A standalone deterministic PoI/PoInsight benchmark and reproducibility harness over static CSV fixtures. Research surface: it has no live finalized-receipt feed and no protocol reputation or incentive integration; its composite reputation number is a local research computation, not protocol law.

evidence.note: afi-bench CLI; two golden-plot tests fail pre-existing

evidence.ref: research-institute-reference-services-v0.1 (INST-GOV D-INST-6 internal trusted boundary; public access reserved to the designated, not-deployed Institute oracle-ingress)

interfaces.name: Factory agent operation registry
interfaces.addressOrOperation: factory.capabilities.list, factory.plugins.list, factory.official.list, factory.template.create/validate/inspect/instantiate, factory.pipeline.validate/inspect, factory.analystConfig.create/validate, factory.plugin.scaffold, factory.artifact.hash/package (14; 12 read-only, 2 mutating)

interfaces.name: Factory MCP stdio adapter
interfaces.addressOrOperation: afi-factory agent serve --transport stdio (initialize, tools/list, tools/call, ping)

interfaces.name: Canonical evidence persistence admission boundary
interfaces.transport: in-process typed methods over the MongoDB driver (no HTTP surface)

evidence.note: the sole mutation boundary; not an HTTP surface
least-privilege BYOK resolution; keyless lanes never touch the resolver
boot-validated provider->strategy binding; honest 403 on absence
authored composition; byte-identical to the afi-config registry record
in-process typed submit to afi-infra; D1->D2 handoff

evidence.ref: CHAIN-GOV (reserved); afi-token 86B emissions on Base Sepolia testnet only, no settlement wiring

repositories.role: Accepted protocol decisions (authority ledger); not runtime
Canonical schemas, registries, KATs, conventions; hosts this Atlas registry
Governed deterministic math kernels + golden vectors (math authority)
Canonical scoring library (UWR engine, profile loader, decay)
Signal-evaluation runtime (District One) + Evidence V3 construction (District Two law)
Canonical evidence store (sole writer, District Two)
Bounded first-party aiMl orchestration + pattern kernel service
Pipeline authoring system + agent capability layer (not an executor)
Off-chain mint/emissions orchestration (on-chain not wired); reserved settlement domain
On-chain token (86B hard cap xERC20); Base Sepolia testnet; reserved settlement domain
Documentation hub; single authoritative current architecture map

participantRoles.name: Signal provider
participantRoles.purpose: Submits trading signals to AFI via the authenticated Gateway boundary or the internal-trusted CPJ route; bound to a registered provider->strategy binding.

participantRoles.name: Analyst / scorer
participantRoles.purpose: Configures and registers analyst strategies (the analystId+strategyId+strategyVersion triple) and the compositions the Reactor executes; a conforming pipeline needs no bespoke permission.

participantRoles.name: Data / enrichment provider
participantRoles.purpose: Supplies a governed ProviderInstance/adapter for one of the five enrichment categories, optionally BYOK; the credential is never in an artifact.

participantRoles.name: Validator
participantRoles.purpose: Validates scored-signal evidence and quality. A named Settlement-doctrine reward track, but no dedicated accepted role-definition decision exists; role weights and participant rights are reserved to CHAIN-GOV.
participantRoles.governanceBasis: reserved -- CHAIN-GOV (role weights / participant rights unfiled); Settlement v1 doctrine names a Validator reward track (draft closure)

participantRoles.name: Operator
participantRoles.purpose: Operates reference services (the hosted Gateway and oracle-ingress/CPJ reference instances). The AFI Research Institute is the designated non-exclusive operator; operating confers no protocol, economic, or validator authority; independent conforming operators are permanently permitted.

participantRoles.name: Builder / developer
participantRoles.purpose: Builds against the governed contracts and the Factory authoring surfaces; conformance is defined by the contracts, registries, and KATs -- no permission is required to build a conforming implementation.

participantRoles.name: Application integrator
participantRoles.purpose: Integrates AFI capabilities into an external application through the Gateway and/or SDK surfaces. A self-serve onboarding control plane does not yet exist; integration today is through the conforming contracts and reference interfaces.
participantRoles.governanceBasis: district-api-atlas-foundation-v0.1 (ATLAS-GOV) discoverability; no onboarding control plane exists

evidence.ref: no onboarding control plane exists; binding is a registry update (FCP-GOV D-FCP-5)

audienceDescriptors.name: Investor
audienceDescriptors.note: A non-conformance audience descriptor only. 'Investor' is NOT an AFI protocol-conformance role and implies no protocol activation, validator/provider privilege, or economic authority (ATLAS-GOV D-ATLAS-10).

audienceDescriptors.name: Researcher
audienceDescriptors.note: A non-conformance audience descriptor: consumes afi-benchkit, afi-artifacts, and afi-docs. Implies no protocol activation or conformance role.

audienceDescriptors.name: Ecosystem observer
audienceDescriptors.note: A non-conformance audience descriptor for ecosystem observers; implies no protocol activation or conformance role.
